title: Caterpillar: 5 Reasons Why You Should Consider Buying This Blue-Chip Aristocrat

The company has a conservative growth outlook with steady earnings and cash flow growth expected in the next two years. title: Caterpillar's stock leads the Dow's premarket losers after Deere's disappointing outlook
text: Shares of Caterpillar Inc. CAT fell 2.7% to pace the Dow Jones Industrial Average's DJIA premarket decliners Wednesday, following fellow construction equipment maker Deere & Co.'s DE disappointing full-year outlook. The implied price decline of Caterpillar's stock would shave about 44 points off the Dow's price, while Dow futures YM00 rose 28 points, or 0.1%, ahead of the open and S&P 500 futures ES00 gained 0.2%.

title: Caterpillar: Valuation Looks Reasonable, With Strong Revenue And Cash Flows
text: With revenue growth per share of 8.4% over the last 5 years, Caterpillar looks like a great growth company at a reasonable 14.3x TTM P/E price. Caterpillar's latest quarterly results show a 12% increase in revenues to $16.8 billion, with EPS up an even more impressive 40.8%. The company has superb cash flows, with capital expenditures only averaging 35% of cash flows from operations and no material acquisitions needed to achieve the solid growth rates.
